name: PowerPlan Fixed Asset & Tax Accounting API
what_it_does: PowerPlan REST API for fixed asset management, tax accounting, and lease accounting platform targeting regulated utilities and asset-intensive industries. Enables AI agents to manage fixed asset record creation, depreciation calculation, and asset lifecycle tracking for financial reporting, handle tax depreciation and deferred tax calculation data for tax accounting automation, access utility rate case and regulatory accounting data for public utility commission filings, retrieve FERC (Federal Energy Regulatory Commission) accounting and financial reporting data for energy companies, handle lease accounting and ASC 842/IFRS 16 compliance data for balance sheet lease recognition, access capital project accounting and asset capitalization data for construction-in-progress (CIP) management, retrieve goodwill and intangible asset amortization data for purchase price allocation, manage property tax and tax provision data for tax compliance, handle regulatory depreciation and revenue requirement calculations for utility rate cases, and integrate asset accounting data with ERP, Oracle, and SAP financial systems.
use_cases: Automating depreciation calculations from asset accounting agents
Tracking lease obligations from ASC 842 compliance agents
Filing FERC accounting data from utility regulatory agents
Managing CIP capitalization from capital project accounting agents
not_for: Simple fixed asset tracking without regulated utility or complex tax accounting
SMB accounting without enterprise asset-intensive industry complexity
Non-asset-intensive industries without complex depreciation and tax needs
best_when: A regulated utility, pipeline company, or asset-intensive manufacturer using PowerPlan wants AI agents to automate fixed asset depreciation, tax provision calculations, FERC regulatory accounting, lease accounting (ASC 842), and capital project capitalization.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: PowerPlan manages tax and regulatory accounting data with direct financial statement impact — automated depreciation method changes or asset impairment without tax and accounting team review creates financial reporting errors and tax compliance risk. FERC accounting automation for regulated utilities requires regulatory affairs team validation. ASC 842 lease modification automation without accounting team approval creates financial statement compliance issues.

auth.notes: PowerPlan uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Per-tenant credentials. No webhooks — batch processing model for depreciation calculations. REST API with JSON. Atlanta, Georgia HQ. Part of Roper Technologies portfolio. Strong in regulated utilities (electric, gas, water), pipeline, and communications. FERC, GAAP, and IFRS accounting standards. Competes with specialized fixed asset software and SAP/Oracle ERP built-in modules for utility asset accounting.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; asset count and module pricing
pricing.notes: Atlanta, Georgia. Part of Roper Technologies (NASDAQ: ROP). Founded 1993. 300+ utility and energy company clients. Manages $1T+ in asset base. Strong in regulated electric, gas, water utilities, pipelines. FERC accounting a key differentiator. Tax depreciation (MACRS, bonus depreciation) and provision calculations. ASC 842/IFRS 16 lease accounting module. Competes with Oracle Assets and SAP Asset Management for utility market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; batch processing has separate limits

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Depreciation method changes affect financial statements and tax returns; automated depreciation parameter changes without tax/accounting team review creates financial reporting errors

FERC accounting regulatory complexity — FERC Uniform System of Accounts has specific account coding requirements for regulated utilities; automated FERC account assignments must comply with FERC chart of accounts
Batch depreciation processing — PowerPlan depreciation runs are batch processes, not real-time; API interactions for depreciation triggering and result retrieval follow batch job patterns
Tax depreciation elections — bonus depreciation and Section 179 elections have annual tax strategy implications; automated asset capitalization must coordinate with tax department
Rate case regulatory data — utility rate case data in PowerPlan has public utility commission (PUC) filing implications; automated changes to regulatory asset data require regulatory affairs review

ASC 842 lease modifications — lease modification accounting (e.g., lease extension, partial termination) has complex measurement requirements; automated lease modifications must apply correct remeasurement logic
agent_readiness.security_notes: Fixed asset and tax accounting. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2/API key. US. Utility asset, depreciation, and tax data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Fixed asset accounting. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Utility asset and tax data.
